thesis: Backblaze is a cloud storage and backup provider with a fast-growing, higher-margin B2 Cloud Storage segment (28% ARR growth) and a transparent, no-egress-fee pricing model. Q1 2026 showed margin expansion to 61%, EPS profitability, and positive operating cash flow. AI/ML storage demand is an emerging tailwind. Rated SPEC. BUY based on B2 growth and margin expansion, balanced against hyperscaler competition and legacy backup drag.

drivers: B2 Cloud Storage accelerates to 35%+ growth as AI/ML workload adoption broadens
Gross margin expands to 65%+ on operational leverage and mix shift
Path to profitability by FY2027 with sustained positive FCF
Partnership announcements (CDN, edge compute) expand TAM and distribution
Analyst re-ratings drive multiple expansion toward 5-6x ARR

assumptions: B2 sustains 15-20% growth; Computer Backup stabilizes
Gross margin holds 60-62% with gradual improvement
Breakeven achieved by end of FY2026
Stock re-rates modestly to ~4x ARR as profitability story gains credibility

risks: B2 growth stalls under hyperscaler price competition (AWS S3, Google Cloud, Azure)
Legacy Computer Backup decline accelerates, offsetting B2 gains
Cash burn resumes if margin expansion slows; dilution risk from capital raise
Small scale ($38M quarterly revenue) makes BLZE vulnerable to customer churn
SMB segment concentration creates volatility in bookings

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — critical for B2 growth sustainability confirmation
AI/ML workload adoption metrics and customer wins
Path to positive FCF / profitability milestone

key_risks: Hyperscaler price competition (AWS S3, Google Cloud, Azure)
Legacy Computer Backup decline offsetting B2 growth
Small scale — $38M quarterly revenue vs $10B+ competitors
Cash burn history; need sustained profitability
Dilution risk if capital raise needed
Customer concentration / churn in SMB segment
